title: payment — kill switches
description: Operational kill switches distinct from the tier-based `features.json` gates. These are for emergency-off / staged rollout / per-tenant ops interventions. Every flag is ON in normal production; off flips are ops decisions.

flags.description: Master toggle for the capture path. Off = new PaymentIntent creation refused. Emergency-only (used during DB migrations or provider-wide incidents).
Master toggle for inbound webhook processing. Off = incoming webhooks return 202 without dispatching to handlers. Used when a provider fires runaway webhooks (rare).
Off = nightly ReconcilePaymentIntentsJob skips. Only flip off for maintenance windows.
Off = ExpireStalePaymentIntentsJob skips. Used when a mass-expiry would cause downstream cancellation floods.
Off = CollectDisputeEvidenceJob skips. Dispute rows still created but evidence not auto-collected. Ops manually collects.
Off = PaymentMethodExpiringNotification + PaymentDisputeEvidenceDueNotification + PaymentReconcileDriftNotification all no-op. Used when notifications module is degraded.
Per-provider kill switch. Off = Stripe captures skip (returns 503 payment_circuit_breaker_open). Used when Stripe is announced-degraded.
Off = PaymentMethod.provider_reference_id + PaymentIntent.provider_reference_id stored in plaintext. NEVER flip off in production (compliance violation — leaked tokens enable fraudulent charges).
Off = PaymentIntent.client_secret stored in plaintext. NEVER flip off in production (compliance violation).
Off = Payment.provider_metadata stored in plaintext. NEVER flip off in production.
Off = FraudChecker returns 'low' risk for every payment. Emergency-only if provider's fraud service is malfunctioning + blocking legitimate captures.
Off = ThreeDsGate always allows without challenge. NEVER flip off in production — PSD2 compliance violation in EU regions.
